preserveUserBrands: assets/brands/* except assets/brands/example/ (example is updatable, user brands are not)

featureFlags._doc: Runtime feature flags read at Electron startup. Do NOT rely on these for security-critical defaults — a stale version.json on an offline client stays at the shipped values. Security-critical changes must land as code pushes with a version bump.

notes: v1.22.2: Comprehensive batch release covering 6 PRs since v1.22.0 — Windows path case-fix, 9:16 image format, archive scroll persistence with follow-new-content, Mailchimp Marketing API integration with full Klaviyo-parity surface, 5 post-audit security hardenings, and 13 UX polish items. Anchor: 2026-05-12. WINDOWS PATH CASE-MISMATCH: pre-fix the merlin:// protocol handler used JavaScript String.startsWith for filesystem containment checks. On Windows where the filesystem is case-insensitive but JS strings are not, os.homedir() and process.env.USERPROFILE could return different cases for the same canonical path — a 403-Forbidden landed on every image-card load and the existing retry-on-error logic could not recover (the 403 was permanent). New pathContains and pathEquals helpers do case-insensitive comparison on win32 only. Byte-exact on macOS/Linux. IMAGE FORMAT 9:16: first-class story/vertical aspect ratio support — pre-fix imageFormat "9:16" silently coerced to the Feed-default (4:5 + 1:1) because neither the primary-aspect map nor the formats switch in runImagePipeline recognized the token. Single canonicalImageFormat alias-fold switch now handles portrait/4:5, square/1:1, story/9:16/vertical/reel/reels, landscape/16:9, both (default), all. Unknown tokens log a warning and fall back to "both" rather than silently coercing. ARCHIVE SCROLL PERSISTENCE: pre-fix alt-tab away from Merlin with the archive sidebar open caused the panel to snap to the top on return. Root cause: fs.watch events queued while unfocused fired on focus regain, the archive-changed IPC triggered loadArchive(), which did innerHTML wipe and rebuilt. New MutationObserver pattern captures scrollTop before the wipe and restores it after cards land + double rAF for layout. Filter-button / search / refresh-button paths reset to top (correct UX for explicit content change). 2026-05-12 follow-up: user within 100px of the bottom auto-snaps to the new bottom on reload (Slack/iMessage convention) so new content arriving while the user is at the tail gets pushed into view rather than missed. MAILCHIMP INTEGRATION (20 actions, full Klaviyo parity): Marketing API key auth (no OAuth — private API keys cover every scope today), tile in #brand-tiles, Slack/Discord tiles visually grouped into the brand section. Action surface: status / audiences / campaigns / performance (read), templates list/get/create/update/delete/bulk-upload, campaigns create/set-content/send-test/send/schedule/delete, automations list/emails/pause/start. CheckMailchimpCampaignCANSPAM gates every send + schedule (5 rules: missing subject/from_name/reply_to/UNSUB-tag/physical-address). applyMailchimpMergeTags swaps the same generic placeholders Klaviyo accepts so the same source HTML works on either platform. POST-AUDIT SECURITY HARDENING (5 fixes): (1) P0 path-traversal in mailchimp-templates-bulk-upload — cmd.Dir now routed through validateBulkUploadDir (filepath.Abs + EvalSymlinks + Rel containment + dot-dot reject); (2) P0 Bash approval-bypass — new BASH_CARDED_DESTRUCTIVE set in main.js parallels the MCP CARDED_DESTRUCTIVE_ACTIONS tier so mailchimp-campaign-send/schedule/automation-pause/start fire an approval card on both the MCP and Bash routes; (3) HIGH Mailchimp token redaction gap — new word-boundary-anchored 32-hex-dash-dc pattern in both Go and JS redactors covers Mailchimp keys (35-37 chars, missed by the existing 40+-char opaque sweep); (4) HIGH CAN-SPAM physical-address heuristic-bypass — tightened from 2-of-N keywords to 3-of-N clustered within 200 chars; (5) MEDIUM strconv.Atoi replaces fmt.Sscanf in runMailchimpCampaignSetContent. UX POLISH (post-audit, 7 items): Mailchimp API-key URL switched to login.mailchimp.com (auto-routes to user datacenter); CAN-SPAM error messages rewritten for the 5th-grader test (no legal-cite jargon, no internal field names, recovery hints included); approval card labels for 12 Mailchimp destructive actions are sentence-level on both Bash and MCP paths (Send this email campaign to your Mailchimp audience not mailchimp-campaign-send); archive follow-new-content convention; pagination on every list-style Mailchimp runner (templates / campaigns / automations) with offset + short-page terminator; empty-state copy rewritten for the 5th-grader test. NEW APPROVAL TIER: CARDED_DESTRUCTIVE_ACTIONS sits between READ_ONLY_ACTIONS (auto-approve) and SPEND_ACTIONS (card with budget context). For destructive non-spend writes that touch live customer-facing state (email blasts, workflow pause/resume), the new tier shows a generic confirmation card with no budget context. Pre-fix these actions auto-approved under the catch-all — a compliant email blast to the WRONG audience is still a fire-the-CEO event regardless of CAN-SPAM passing. TEST COVERAGE: 14 new security tests in mailchimp_security_test.go, 11 new polish tests in mailchimp_polish_test.go, regression guards for path traversal, bash bypass, Mailchimp redaction (Go + JS), CAN-SPAM 3-of-N clustering, strconv.Atoi rejection, pagination two-page aggregation, CAN-SPAM copy quality (no banned substrings + recovery hint), template name-only/html-only update, delete/automation 200 vs 204 path, summarize empty/empty-JSON. All 1173 autoCMO tests + Go merlin/facts/factverify all pass.
whatsNew: Chat image cards render correctly on Windows installs (case-mismatch path fix)
9:16 vertical image generation (Instagram Stories / Reels / TikTok / Shorts) as a first-class format
Mailchimp integration — full Klaviyo-parity surface (20 actions: templates CRUD + bulk-upload, campaign send / schedule, automation pause/resume)
Archive sidebar keeps your scroll position when external files appear or you alt-tab away
5 post-audit security hardenings + 13 UX polish items (login URL, CAN-SPAM copy, approval-card labels, pagination)
